name: camelot-py (PDF Table Extraction)

performance.notes: pdfminer-based text extraction + OpenCV image analysis; complex PDFs slower

agent_readiness.notes: Extract tables from PDFs as pandas DataFrames. import camelot. tables = camelot.read_pdf('invoice.pdf'). → TableList. tables[0].df → pandas DataFrame. tables[0].parsing_report → accuracy, whitespace, order. Flavors: 'lattice' (lines/borders, needs OpenCV/ghostscript), 'stream' (whitespace-separated, text-only PDFs). camelot.read_pdf('file.pdf', flavor='stream', pages='1,2,3'). export: tables[0].to_csv('table.csv'). to_excel, to_json, to_html. Compound pages: pages='1-3,5'. Requires Ghostscript + OpenCV for lattice flavor. tabula-py is a Java-based alternative.

gotchas: Lattice flavor needs Ghostscript (apt/brew) and OpenCV (pip install camelot-py[cv])
Works best on text-based PDFs; scanned PDFs need OCR preprocessing
Accuracy varies by PDF — always check parsing_report.accuracy
tabula-py (Java-based) may work better on some PDFs
Package name is camelot-py on PyPI (not camelot which is a game library)
best_when: Extracting tables from text-based PDFs (invoices, financial reports)
PDFs with visible grid lines (lattice flavor)
When tabula-py's Java dependency is undesirable
summary: camelot-py extracts PDF tables as pandas DataFrames. import camelot; tables = camelot.read_pdf('invoice.pdf', flavor='lattice'); df = tables[0].df; print(tables[0].parsing_report). Flavors: lattice (grid lines, needs OpenCV/Ghostscript) or stream (whitespace). Pages: pages='1,2-5'. MIT. Best for text-based PDFs with tables. Not for scanned PDFs. tabula-py for Java-based alternative.
